title: GIF to JPG Converter Free | Everything PDF
metaDescription: Convert GIF to JPG for free. Pick your quality, get a ZIP for batches. Everything is processed right in your browser, so it stays private.
subtitle: Convert GIF images to JPG. Animated GIFs keep only the first frame, transparent areas fill with white. Choose your quality, free.
intro: This is the tool for when you want to turn a GIF into a widely compatible, lightweight JPG. Animated GIFs are saved as a JPG of the first frame only, and since JPG doesn't support transparency, any transparent areas are filled with white. You can choose the quality to balance file size against sharpness, and if you drop in several images at once, they all come back bundled in a single ZIP. Nothing is ever uploaded anywhere; it's all processed right on your own device, so you can convert with confidence.
steps: Drag in the GIF images you want to convert, or add them with [Choose Files]. You can do several at once.
Pick the quality you want. Higher means sharper but larger files.
Click [Convert to JPG]. Transparent areas are filled with white.
Download a single JPG, or a ZIP if you converted several.
security: The entire process of converting and saving your GIF as a JPG happens inside your own browser, and no file is ever sent to a server. That means you can convert work files and personal images without any upload worries.

faq.a: For an animated GIF, only the first frame is saved as a still JPG image. JPG can't hold motion, so the animation isn't preserved. Think of it as converting to a single still picture.

JPG doesn't support transparency, so any areas that were transparent in the GIF get filled with white. If you need to keep the transparent background intact, use [GIF to PNG] instead of JPG. PNG preserves transparency.

faq.q: How do I choose the quality?
faq.a: Higher quality means a sharper image but a larger file; lower quality means a lighter file with less detail. We recommend medium quality for web uploads or email attachments, and high quality for printing or archiving. Adjust it to fit your use.

faq.q: Can I convert several files at once?
faq.a: Yes, upload multiple GIFs at once and they'll all be converted to JPG and bundled into a single ZIP for download. No need to save them one by one, you can handle them all in one go.

faq.q: Is it really free? Any watermarks or usage limits?
faq.a: It's completely free with no watermarks. There are no conversion limits, file size caps, or sign-ups. Convert GIF to JPG freely, whenever you need to.

extraSections.heading: When this comes in handy
extraSections.body: It's perfect for when you only have a GIF but need to post it to a board or form that only accepts JPG, or for shrinking the file size to send by email. Since animated GIFs keep only their first frame and transparent areas turn white, it's a great fit when you need a single still image to represent the file. If you need to preserve a transparent background, use the PNG conversion instead.
